rulings.Case Title: ENKHDUL BATSUKH VS. A-NHI LE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Sanctions Of $1935
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep. 12, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANTs A-NHI LE and QU KUANG's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Sanctions Of $1935 IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: (1) WITHIN TWENTY (20) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO KUANG VERIFIED FULL AND COMPLETE, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO KUANG'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 2 - 6. THE MERE RECITATION OF THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT IS NOT A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO COMPLY WITH THE OBLIGATIONS OF THE CODE (2) WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO KUANG WITHOUT OBJECTION FULL AND COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY.17.1 AS TO EACH DENIAL OF KUANG'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION. (3) KUANG'S MOTION IS DENIED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 1.1. (4) SANCTIONS GRANTED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS ($1,000.00 PAYABLE TO KUANG AND HIS ATTORNEY WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.= (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ENKHDUL BATSUKH VS. A-NHI LE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant A-Nhi Le'S Form Interrogatories, Set One- Seven; Request For Sanctions Of $3285 Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep. 12, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT A-NHI LE and QU KUANG's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant A-Nhi Le'S Form Interrogatories, Set One- Seven; Request For Sanctions Of $3285 Against Plaintiff DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is DENIED to the extent that it seeks duplicative responses based on the definition of "Incident". Motion is GRANTED as follows: Plaintiff to deliver within twenty (20) calendar days from the date of this Order to Defendant Le without objection full and complete verified code compliant responses to Le's Form Interrogatories Nos 2.6, 2.7, 2.12, 2.13, 7.2, 7.3. 50.1, 50.2, 50.4, &50.5. Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of two-thousand-dollar ($2,000.00) against Plaintiff and her counsel payable to Le and her counsel within ten (10) calendar days from the date of this Order. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

DEFENDANTs A-NHI LE and QU KUANG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant A-Nhi Le'S Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Sanctions Of $3532 GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) WITHIN TWENTY (20) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO LE, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, VERIFIED FULL AND COMPLETE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO EACH OF LE'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. DEFENDANTs A-NHI LE and QU KUANG Motion To Deem Admitted The Truth Of All Matters Specified In Defendant'S Requests For Admission, Set One; Or To Compel Further Responses; Request For Sanctions Of $4275 GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: (1) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO LE, VERIFIED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES EITHER ADMITTING OR DENYING THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSION NOS. 17 - 24. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
